title: iberis lagascana var. badalii
intro: Iberis lagascana var. badalii, also known as Candytuft, is a flowering plant species in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its white, pink, or purple flowers and compact, shrub-like growth. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, reducing inflammation, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Candytuft include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, calm coughs, and promote overall well-being. Additionally, Candytuft has been traditionally used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections due to its expectorant properties.
